_meta.report: ING Group Annual Report 2025 (ing-2025-12-31-en.xhtml → 89,783 lines clean text)
_meta.method: Full XHTML → clean text conversion (121MB → 1.86M chars), then reading of key sections: L19716-23275 (financed+facilitated emissions), L79111-79310 (technical notes), L17951-18150 (value chain), L25381-25580 (PCAF membership check).
_meta.corrections_vs_partial_read: 2 errors corrected: Asset Class Coverage 5→4 (sovereign confirmed missing after full search, 88.9%<90%), Temporal Coverage 1→3 (full report reveals 2018-2025 Terra tracking + 2030 targets)

Listed equity and corporate bonds.verbatim: L23014-23073: Equity investments 4.5 EUR bn, 58 ktCO2e S1&2, 1,438 S3, DQS 4.9 | Corporate bonds 0.2 EUR bn, 251 ktCO2e S1&2, 569 S3, DQS 3.6

Business loans and unlisted equity.verbatim: L22642-22701: Corporate lending WB 206.0 EUR bn, 43,503 ktCO2e S1&2, 186,488 S3, DQS 4.0 | Corporate lending BB 84.8 EUR bn, 13,373 ktCO2e S1&2, 50,351 S3, DQS 5.0

Project finance.verbatim: L22704-22950: Terra sectors within WB Corporate lending: Power Generation 13.4 EUR bn, Oil&Gas, Cement 0.6, Steel 1.8, Aviation 3.0, Shipping 4.2 — all with S1&2 and S3 separately

Commercial real estate.verbatim: L22566-22640: Commercial Real Estate WB 21.7 EUR bn, 420 ktCO2e, DQS 3.7 | BB 14.5 EUR bn, 217 ktCO2e, DQS 3.3

Mortgages.verbatim: L22547-22565: Residential Mortgages 372.9 EUR bn, 5,759 ktCO2e, DQS 3.9

Motor vehicle loans.verbatim: L22859-22879: Terra: Automotive 4.1 EUR bn, 62 ktCO2e S1&2, 5,373 S3, DQS 2.5. Note: this covers OEM manufacturers, not consumer auto loans. Consumer motor vehicle lending likely included within BB corporate lending but not separately identified as a PCAF asset class.

Sovereign debt.verbatim: [CONFIRMED after full report search] Sovereign bonds appear in value chain (L18028, L18038: 'Sovereign, sub-sovereign and agencies' under Treasury & Other investments) and in financial context (L87787) but NOT in the financed emissions table (L22547-23075). No sovereign debt financed emissions calculated.

2_Data_Quality_Score.detail: L79181-79194: 'We present the weighted average data quality score per asset class or sector. [...] PCAF data quality score 1 and 2 that currently represent a range of 11 to 21 percent of the financed emissions across scope 1, 2 and 3 in our business loans.' Per asset class: Residential Mortgages 3.9, CRE WB 3.7, CRE BB 3.3, Corporate BB 5.0, Corporate WB 4.0, Equity 4.9, Bonds 3.6.
2_Data_Quality_Score.scoring_grid: Weighted average DQS 4.0 falls in range 3.5–4.5 → Score 2

3_Attribution_Methodology.pcaf_signatory: UNCONFIRMED in full report (89,783 lines searched). L25475 'founding signatory' refers to UNEP FI Financial Health Commitment, not PCAF. ING is publicly known as PCAF co-founder but this is not stated in the 2025 AR.
3_Attribution_Methodology.verbatim: L22328-22330: 'Both metrics are calculated in accordance with the Partnership for Carbon Accounting Financials (PCAF) methodology to ensure transparency and comparability.' L19966-19968: 'ING became the first global systemically important bank to receive validation from the Science Based Targets initiative (SBTi) for 1.5°C-aligned targets.'
3_Attribution_Methodology.scoring_rationale: Comprehensive PCAF implementation across all asset classes + SBTi 1.5°C validation + PCAF DQS scoring applied. Part A status = reported. Score 4.

4_Portfolio_Coverage.verbatim: L23076-23096: 'Total financing | 704.5' | 'Financing not in scope of financed emissions | 187.5' | 'Total on-balance (IFRS 9 Eligible) in gross carrying amount | 892.0'
4_Portfolio_Coverage.explanation_of_exclusions: L22420-22428: 'The difference of €187.5 billion [...] is explained by the exclusion of products such as money market instruments and pre-settlements (€179 billion), lending to private individuals other than retail mortgages (€18 billion) and accounting adjustments such as accrued interest (€-9 billion). These exposures either do not represent long term financing [...] or fall outside the PCAF methodology.'

5_Temporal_Coverage.years_available: PCAF table: 2024-2025. Terra tracking: 2018-2025. SBTi reference year: 2021.
5_Temporal_Coverage.verbatim: L19724: 'Established in 2018, Terra prioritises the sectors...' | L19973: '67 percent of our total financed emissions for reference year 2021' | L20068: '71% reduction since 2019' | Targets: 2030 targets for all Terra sectors + 2050 net zero.
5_Temporal_Coverage.scoring_rationale: ≥3 years history (2018 baseline → 2025) + trends (annual reductions tracked across 9 sectors) + projections/targets (2030 SBTi-validated targets + 2050 net zero). Score 3.

1_Asset_Class_Coverage.verbatim: L23138-23154: 'Facilitated emissions relate to emissions connected to transactions we arrange or facilitate, such as syndicated loans [...] We identified syndicated loans as material and therefore report facilitated emissions for this activity.' DCM/ECM excluded: 'total facilitated emissions from DCM/ECM being below 2.5 percent of our reported financed Scope 1 and 2 emissions.'
1_Asset_Class_Coverage.detail: 1 of 5 Part B asset classes (syndicated loans only). Score ~2/10 raw → 20% → Level 1.

2_Attribution_Methodology.verbatim: L23153-23154: 'calculated using the PCAF methodology for facilitated emissions, applying the prescribed weighting factor of 33 percent.' L23235-23264: Syndicated loans 45.4 EUR bn, 1,598 ktCO2e S1&2, DQS 3.9; 12,268 ktCO2e S3, DQS 4.0.
2_Attribution_Methodology.scoring_rationale: PCAF Part B methodology applied with standard weighting factor. Score 3.

3_Temporal_Coverage.verbatim: Facilitated emissions table shows 2025 and 2024 columns only.

Part_C.reason: ING is a bank, not insurer/reinsurer/bancassurance.

Comparison_partial_vs_full_read.Asset_Class_Coverage: 5 → 4 (sovereign confirmed missing after searching 89,783 lines; 88.9% < 90% threshold)
Comparison_partial_vs_full_read.Temporal_Coverage: 1 → 3 (full report reveals Terra tracking since 2018, baselines from 2019-2023, 2030 targets — invisible in 150K-char partial read)
Comparison_partial_vs_full_read.conclusion: Full reading corrected 2 errors. Both caused by missing context outside the partial read window. The XHTML→clean text approach (121MB→1.86M chars, 99% reduction) made full reading feasible.
